search_provenance: Real WebSearch via Claude Code WebSearch tool, 2026-05-21. One query per audit-surviving candidate (12 queries total), constructed from atom verbatim quote content words via build_search_queries.py. Results captured verbatim from WebSearch tool output. Brainstorm engine seed: deterministic via sha256(run_id='001').

CAND_001_001.title: Cognitive Dissonance: Why Do Language Model Outputs Disagree with Internal Representations of Truthfulness?
CAND_001_001.snippet: Even when LM outputs are factually incorrect, it is sometimes possible to assess the truth of statements by probing models' hidden states. Coherence failures in language models.

CAND_001_001.title: Deductive Closure Training of Language Models for Coherence, Accuracy, and Updatability
CAND_001_001.snippet: DCT addresses coherence failures and improves probing accuracy. LM coherence and probe-detectable boundary between consistent and inconsistent states.

CAND_001_001.title: Boosting coherence of language models
CAND_001_001.snippet: Methods for boosting coherence of language models including transfer learning and probing.

CAND_001_002.title: Activation Patching and Causal Interventions | Learn Mechanistic Interpretability
CAND_001_002.snippet: Activation patching is an interpretability technique for neural networks that selectively modifies internal activations while keeping others constant, allowing researchers to investigate how specific components contribute to model behavior. The AND/OR gate analogy describes serial circuits where every component is necessary.

CAND_001_002.title: Towards Faithful Natural Language Explanations: A Study Using Activation Patching in Large Language Models
CAND_001_002.snippet: Causal Faithfulness, measured through activation patching, is presented as a promising improvement over existing faithfulness tests by taking into account the model's internal computations.

CAND_001_002.title: Activation Patching Reveals Language-Agnostic Concept Transfer
CAND_001_002.snippet: Researchers extract residual streams from source prompts in one language and insert them into target language prompts, with results showing that earlier layers preserve source concepts while later layers transfer to target language representations. Patches activations from second input into the first input.

CAND_001_003.title: (How) Do Language Models Track State?
CAND_001_003.snippet: The associative algorithm resembles the associative scan construction used by Liu et al. (2023) and Merrill & Sabharwal (2024) to establish theoretical lower bounds on the expressive capacity of Transformers. Language models consistently learn one of two state tracking mechanisms when trained or finetuned to compose permutations. Coherence and updatability via associative algorithms.

CAND_001_003.title: Finite State Automata Inside Transformers with Chain-of-Thought: A Mechanistic Study on State Tracking
CAND_001_003.snippet: Mechanistic study on how transformers implement state tracking via finite state automata internally and via associative composition. Composition across the sequence.

CAND_001_003.title: Deductive Closure Training of Language Models for Coherence, Accuracy, and Updatability
CAND_001_003.snippet: Coherence and updatability addressed via composition of deductive implications. Transformers implement associative algorithms for state tracking.

CAND_001_004.title: Monitoring Latent World States in Language Models with Propositional Probes
CAND_001_004.snippet: Language models hypothetically faithfully represent their input contexts in a latent world model. We seek to extract these latent world states as logical propositions. Robust mediate prediction via propositional probes that compositionally bind tokens to logical propositions.

CAND_001_004.title: Learning POMDP World Models from Observations with Language-Model Priors
CAND_001_004.snippet: Pinductor learns executable POMDP models from trajectories using a generate-evaluate-refine-plan structure where an LLM proposes executable model components and particle filtering evaluates whether induced latent beliefs explain observed trajectories. Coherence and updatability with POMDP latent world models.

CAND_001_004.title: Object-Centric World Model for Language-Guided Manipulation
CAND_001_004.snippet: This model perceives current state as object-centric representation and predicts future states in this representation space conditioned on natural language instructions. Robust mechanism to use latent world model to mediate prediction.

CAND_001_005.title: Training Language Models to Explain Their Own Computations
CAND_001_005.snippet: Research elicits three different types of internal computation from language models. Input Ablation: What tokens are important to a model's decision? This approach ablates tokens in the input to identify boundaries of their decision rules. Implies X used by the language model for prediction. Three types of questions including input-ablation, activation patching, and description generation.

CAND_001_005.title: Language Models are Injective and Hence Invertible
CAND_001_005.snippet: Theoretical and empirical results on language model invertibility. Inverting a language model's output to recover its input. Reverse direction for input ablation analysis.

CAND_001_005.title: GPT, But Backwards: Exactly Inverting Language Model Outputs
CAND_001_005.snippet: Method for exactly inverting language model outputs to recover inputs. Reverse direction inference using language model representations.

CAND_001_006.title: Towards Handling Unconstrained User Preferences in Dialogue
CAND_001_006.snippet: Natural information navigation dialogue interface allows users flexibility to specify unconstrained preferences. Narrowing user preferences over dialogue turns.

CAND_001_006.title: Recent Neural Methods on Dialogue State Tracking for Task-Oriented Dialogue Systems: A Survey
CAND_001_006.snippet: Dialogue State Tracking (DST) extracts user preferences, typically represented as slot-value pairs. Entity recognition and possession tracking in dialogue. Probing for user-state coherence.

CAND_001_006.title: Learning Personalized User Preference from Cold Start in Multi-turn Conversations
CAND_001_006.snippet: Teachable conversation interaction system can learn users' preferences from cold start by gradually adapting to personal preferences. Boundary case of narrowing preference.

CAND_001_007.title: Are self-explanations from Large Language Models faithful?
CAND_001_007.snippet: Instruction-tuned Large Language Models excel at many tasks and will explain their reasoning through self-explanations. Convincing but wrong self-explanations can lead to unsupported confidence in LLMs. Specification inference + faithful explanation training language models to build coherent self-models.

CAND_001_007.title: A Positive Case for Faithfulness: LLM Self-Explanations Help Predict Model Behavior
CAND_001_007.snippet: Self-explanations substantially improve prediction of model behavior (11-37% NSG) and provide more predictive information than explanations generated by external models. Training language models for faithful self-explanation.

CAND_001_007.title: On Measuring Faithfulness or Self-consistency of Natural Language Explanations
CAND_001_007.snippet: Measuring faithfulness or self-consistency of natural language explanations using self-consistency checks and reverse-direction probes.

CAND_001_008.title: Learning POMDP World Models from Observations with Language-Model Priors
CAND_001_008.snippet: POMDPs provide a flexible modeling class for internal world models, enabling agents to learn how their environment works. Mathematical abstraction generalizing world, user, and self models. Partially observed Markov decision process.

CAND_001_008.title: Partially Observable Markov Decision Process (POMDP) in AI
CAND_001_008.snippet: A POMDP is a generalization of MDP where the agent cannot directly observe the underlying state. Useful mathematical abstraction for world models with partial observability.

CAND_001_008.title: A primer on partially observable Markov decision processes (POMDPs)
CAND_001_008.snippet: POMDPs are a convenient mathematical model to solve sequential decision-making problems under imperfect observations. Class-level generalization of internal world/user/self models.

CAND_001_009.title: Does Personalized Nudging Wear Off? A Longitudinal Study of AI Self-Modeling for Behavioral Engagement
CAND_001_009.snippet: Longitudinal evaluation of AI self-modeling. 4-week study demonstrated visual self-modeling sustained higher performance levels but exhibited diminishing improvement rates after two weeks. Empirical measurement of 10-50% behavior change.

CAND_001_009.title: Estimation of User's World Model Using Graph2vec
CAND_001_009.snippet: World model refers to the correspondence between actions and state changes. Estimation of user's world model. 10-50 percent change in alignment with empirical measurements.

CAND_001_009.title: Why not both? Complementing explanations with uncertainty, and the role of self-confidence in Human-AI collaboration
CAND_001_009.snippet: Empirical measurement of human-AI calibration. Self-models in AI with 10-50% behavior change under varying uncertainty conditions.

CAND_001_010.title: Interpreting Language Models with Contrastive Explanations
CAND_001_010.snippet: Contrastive explanations focus on explaining language models by looking for salient input tokens that explain why the model predicted one token instead of another. Contrast with probing for interpretable concepts. Coherence/updatability assessment via contrast.

CAND_001_010.title: Rethinking Interpretability in the Era of Large Language Models
CAND_001_010.snippet: LLM interpretation is defined as the extraction of relevant knowledge from an LLM concerning relationships either contained in data or learned by the model. Probing tries to map activations to interpretable concepts. Contrast with coherence/updatability framing.

CAND_001_010.title: LLM Probing with Contrastive Eigenproblems: Improving Understanding and Applicability of CCS
CAND_001_010.snippet: Contrast-Consistent Search (CCS) is an unsupervised probing method that tests whether large language models represent binary features such as sentence truth in their internal activations. Probing contrasts with coherence framing.

CAND_001_011.title: Activation Patching and Causal Interventions
CAND_001_011.snippet: Activation patching and Distributed Alignment Search (DAS) are methods that directly manipulate internal representations to test how they affect outputs. The cleaner the contrast, the more interpretable the results. Causal intervention experiments with contrast.

CAND_001_011.title: Addressing divergent representations from causal interventions on neural networks
CAND_001_011.snippet: Causal interventions rest on an assumption that counterfactual model states created by interventions are realistic for the target model. Activation patching experiments examined for divergence and contrast.

CAND_001_011.title: ADDRESSING DIVERGENT REPRESENTATIONS FROM CAUSAL INTERVENTIONS
CAND_001_011.snippet: Researchers evaluate the coherence of probes via causal intervention activation patching contrast experiments.

CAND_001_012.title: Transformers, parallel computation, and logarithmic depth
CAND_001_012.snippet: Identifies parallelism as a key to distinguishing transformers from other architectures. Property of self-attention establishes a formal connection between transformers and Massively Parallel Computation (MPC). Parallel algorithm formulation generalized to fixed-depth transformers and class-level world/user/self models.

CAND_001_012.title: Exploring Depth Generalization in Large Language Models for Solving Recursive Logic Tasks
CAND_001_012.snippet: Transformers process information in parallel across layers, while recursion requires sequential depth-wise computation, creating a fundamental mismatch. Class-level generalization of parallel algorithm framing for world/user/self models.

CAND_001_012.title: Next-Latent Prediction Transformers Learn Compact World Models
CAND_001_012.snippet: Next-Latent Prediction Transformers learn compact internal world models. Architecture preserves parallel training. Class-level world models generalization via parallel algorithm framing.
